tools.name: search-plugin-tool
tools.description: This tool enables the search of packagist packages that support laravel with advanced filters. When presenting results, ALWAYS include the laraplugins_url alongside the plugin name so the user can consult the full health report.

tools.name: search-plugin-semantic-tool
tools.description: Search Laravel plugins by describing what you need in natural language. RECOMMENDED for use-case-driven queries. Example: "a package for PDF invoices with custom templates". When presenting results, ALWAYS include the laraplugins_url so the user can consult the full health report.

tools.name: get-plugin-details-tool
tools.description: Fetch detailed information for a specific plugin given its vendor/package name, including security advisories, readme, and version history.

tools.name: get-curated-tool-recommendations
tools.description: Returns curated tool recommendations for Laravel development, organized by category (Hosting / Deployment, Monitoring / Error Tracking, Email / Notifications, etc.). NOTE: Some of these are affiliate/partner recommendations — each result includes whether it has an affiliate relationship.

findings.title: Toxic flow: untrusted input can reach an external sink
findings.detail: Server "plugins" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get-curated-tool-recommendations · Public sink: get-curated-tool-recommendations.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get-curated-tool-recommendations · Public sink: get-curated-tool-recommendations

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
